text:
Once upon a time in the quiet village of Tranquil Meadows, there was a peculiar clockmaker named Harrison. Harrison was famous for his ornately carved clocks, but his most extraordinary creation was invisible. It was a small, silver clock that only heard whispers of water dripping from a nearby fountain. Every evening after a thunderstorm, Harrison would listen carefully to that water clock. Once, he convinced it that he could tick and tock just like a living thing. From that day on, he started waking around midnight, patrolling Tranquil Meadows, keeping time and counting sheep for those who missed their melodic snoozes.

One quiet moonlit night, Harrison was doing just that when he heard a curious noise off in the distance. It wasn't water dripping—it sounded more like laughter. Curious, Harrison shuffled over to the fountain. That's when he saw it—a dragon. The dragon was as sleek and graceful as a panther, its eyes twinkling with mirth. Harrison felt
